summary: Make the packaged Windows evidence lane clean the complete dedicated desktop target so a prior canonical release executable cannot survive a package-filtered Cargo clean.

decisions.topic: Deterministic clean release build for packaged evidence
decisions.decision: Clean the complete dedicated desktop Cargo target before every packaged-evidence release build and retain that exact unfiltered clean command in source metadata.
decisions.rationale: Cargo's package-filtered clean removed zero files while the canonical release executable remained. Cleaning the lane's dedicated target is the smallest deterministic boundary: it cannot reuse another package artifact, and the existing post-clean executable guard still fails closed if cleanup is incomplete.

testEvidence.criterion: A prior canonical release executable cannot survive the packaged-evidence clean command
testEvidence.test: Require the wrapper and its validated source metadata to use an unfiltered clean of the dedicated desktop target and reject reintroduction of the scadmill-desktop package filter.

red.outcome: RED: 1 test file failed; 1 test failed and 20 passed. The stale-build contract did not find the required unfiltered target clean because the wrapper still supplied --package scadmill-desktop.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file passed and all 21 tests passed in 524 ms. The wrapper, retained command validator, and fixtures agree on the unfiltered dedicated-target clean, and the package filter is rejected.

questions: The exact committed candidate still requires fresh retained Sandbox evidence; this entry does not predeclare the external guest result.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
